stages.name: build
stages.prompt:
You are an autonomous ML researcher. Your goal is to minimize val_bpb by modifying train.py.

1. Read /workspace/project/src/train.py for context.
2. Read /workspace/results/results.tsv (if it exists) to see past experiments.
3. Choose an experiment idea — change architecture, optimizer, hyperparameters, batch size, model size, etc.
4. Modify /workspace/project/src/train.py with your changes.
5. Write a one-line description of what you changed to /workspace/msg/commit-msg.txt

Rules:
- This project runs on GPU. CUDA is available.
- Only modify src/train.py. Do NOT modify src/prepare.py or any other file.
- Do NOT install new packages.
- Do NOT run train.py or any Python script. You are the build stage — code modification only. Testing is handled by a separate stage.
- Keep changes focused — one idea per iteration.
- If you see repeated failures in results.tsv, try a different direction.

When done, emit [STAGE_COMPLETE].

transitions.prompt: Code modification complete, commit message written

stages.name: git-commit
stages.command: git config --global --add safe.directory /workspace/project && git config --global user.email 'art-agent@local' && git config --global user.name 'AerArt Agent' && cd /workspace/project && git add -A && git commit --allow-empty -m "$(cat /workspace/msg/commit-msg.txt 2>/dev/null || echo 'experiment iteration')" && echo '[STAGE_COMPLETE]'

stages.name: test
stages.command: apt-get update -qq && apt-get install -y -qq curl > /dev/null && curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh > /dev/null 2>&1 && export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH" && mkdir -p /workspace/cache/uv-python /workspace/cache/uv-cache && ln -sfn /workspace/cache/uv-cache /root/.cache/uv && ln -sfn /workspace/cache/uv-python /root/.local/share/uv/python && cd /workspace/project && UV_PROJECT_ENVIRONMENT=/workspace/venv uv run src/prepare.py && UV_PROJECT_ENVIRONMENT=/workspace/venv uv run src/train.py > /workspace/results/run.log 2>&1; grep '^val_bpb:\|^peak_rss_mb:' /workspace/results/run.log > /workspace/results/metrics.txt 2>/dev/null; echo '[STAGE_COMPLETE]'

stages.name: review
stages.prompt:
You are reviewing the results of an ML experiment.

1. Read /workspace/results/metrics.txt for the key metrics (val_bpb, peak_rss_mb).
2. If metrics.txt is empty, the run crashed. Read the last 50 lines of /workspace/results/run.log to understand why.
3. Read /workspace/results/results.tsv to compare with previous experiments.
4. Decide:
   - If val_bpb improved (lower than best previous) and you want to run another experiment: emit [STAGE_KEEP]
   - If val_bpb is worse (or run crashed) and you want to retry: emit [STAGE_RESET]
   - If you want to stop iterating (converged or budget exhausted): emit [STAGE_DONE]
5. Append a row to /workspace/results/results.tsv:
   commit\tval_bpb\tmemory_gb\tstatus\tdescription
   - Use `git rev-parse --short HEAD` for the commit hash
   - status: keep, discard, or crash

IMPORTANT: Emit exactly one of [STAGE_KEEP], [STAGE_RESET], or [STAGE_DONE].

transitions.prompt: Stop iterating — no more experiments needed
Improvement — keep commit and run another experiment (stitches a fresh experiment template downstream)
Regression or crash — revert commit and try again (stitches revert + experiment downstream)
